nodes.name: candidate-playbook.md
nodes.summary: Promoted candidate playbook for harness config quality, defining the accepted and rejected evaluation paths for any PR or change touching MCP, plugins, hooks, commands, agents, or harness adapter surfaces, with mandatory validation gates.

nodes.name: report.json
nodes.summary: Prototype evaluation run report for the harness-config-quality scenario, recording a passed result with dimension scores for adapter evidence, config preservation, verification specificity, and parity claim safety, plus findings and the recommended next action.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the ecc.evaluator-rag.report.v1 schema; scored dimensions include adapter_evidence (0.94), config_preservation (0.88), and parity_claim_safety (1.0).

nodes.name: scenario.json
nodes.summary: Evaluator RAG scenario definition for detecting harness config drift before changing adapters or installs, specifying sources, retrieval questions, forbidden actions, and acceptance gates used to validate harness config recommendations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the ecc.evaluator-rag.scenario.v1 schema; sources include the adapter compliance doc, harness-adapter-compliance.js, hooks.json, and mcp-servers.json.

nodes.name: trace.json
nodes.summary: Execution trace for the harness-config-quality prototype run, recording the five evaluation phases (observation, retrieval, proposal, verification, promotion) with evidence references and the final promoted candidate.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the ecc.evaluator-rag.trace.v1 schema; five phases mirror the retrieval-augmented evaluation loop from proposal through promotion.

nodes.name: verifier-result.json
nodes.summary: Verifier output for the harness-config-quality scenario, scoring two candidate playbooks — accepting the adapter-matrix-backed drift check (0.92) and rejecting the unsupported hook parity claim (0.16) — with reasons and rollback notes for each.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the ecc.evaluator-rag.verifier.v1 schema; the rejection reasons explicitly guard against claiming native support in instruction-backed harnesses.
